prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a single friendly robot stands at the hub of six winding paths that fan outward, each path leading to a tiny vignette of a different world (a stock candlestick mountain, a hospital heartbeat pulse, a spinning factory gear, a sun and wind turbine, a small rover arm, a glowing molecule), the robot calmly holding one slender thread that ties all six together. Style: friendly XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive, one clear visual gag of one method serving many domains, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A robot at the center of six paths each leading to a different industry vignette, holding one thread that connects finance, health, factory, energy, robotics, and science.
caption: One temporal toolkit, six very different industries, all asking the same question about what happens next.

after_heading: 2. The Cardinal Discipline: Leakage and Backtest Overfitting Intermediate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a proud cartoon trader robot admires a dazzling upward curve in a funhouse mirror that flatters it into a soaring mountain, while the real curve in its hand is a flat wiggly line drifting downward, the robot beaming at the reflection and ignoring the truth in its own hand. Style: dry knowing XKCD or Kurzgesagt humor, expressive face, one clear visual gag of a backtest that looked too good to be true, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A trader robot admires a flattering soaring curve in a funhouse mirror while the flat disappointing real curve sits ignored in its hand.
caption: A backtest that leaks the future flatters you in the mirror; the honest out of sample curve is the flat one you were trying not to look at.

after_heading: 1. The Data Reality: Irregular, Multivariate, Missing-Not-at-Random Intermediate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a hospital bedside where a sleeping patient who is doing fine is measured rarely (sparse gentle dots floating above), while a worried nurse robot clusters thickly around a sicker patient taking measurements constantly (dense crowded dots), making clear that the gaps in the data are not random but tell you who was sick. Style: gentle clear Kurzgesagt panel, warm and calm, one readable idea that absence carries information, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A calm patient measured with sparse floating dots beside a sicker patient surrounded by dense clustered measurements, showing that missing data is informative.
caption: In clinical data the gaps are a signal: you get measured more when you are sicker, so missingness itself carries the diagnosis.

after_heading: 2. The Signal-Processing Backbone: Reading Faults in the Spectrum Intermediate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a detective robot holds a prism up to a humming factory machine, and the machine's messy vibration enters as a tangled wiggle and exits the prism fanned into clean separated colored bands like a rainbow, one band glowing brighter to reveal the hidden fault. Style: clear friendly XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, one readable idea that the frequency spectrum separates a fault from the noise, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A detective robot holds a prism to a vibrating machine, splitting its tangled vibration into clean frequency bands with one band glowing to reveal a fault.
caption: A spinning machine hides its faults in plain sight; the frequency spectrum is the prism that fans the hum apart until the broken part stands out.

after_heading: 2. Renewable Generation Forecasting: Selling the Weather's Uncertainty Intermediate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a calm grid operator robot stands beside a wind turbine and a sun, holding not a single thread of the future but a wide handheld paper fan whose blades spread into a range of possible tomorrows, confidently planning around the whole spread rather than betting on one blade. Style: clear friendly Kurzgesagt panel, one readable idea that good forecasts sell a range not a point, warm and tidy, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A grid operator robot holds a paper fan whose blades spread into many possible futures beside a turbine and sun, planning around the whole range.
caption: A renewable forecast worth trusting hands you a fan of possible tomorrows, not a single confident line; the value is in pricing the whole range.

after_heading: 1. Discovering Dynamics From Data: The Method Family Intermediate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a gentle sculptor robot chisels away a cloud of fuzzy scattered data dots, and from the chipped-away noise a clean simple elegant curved law emerges like a statue revealed inside rough stone, the robot stepping back with quiet satisfaction. Style: warm knowing Kurzgesagt or XKCD panel, one readable idea that a sparse governing law hides inside noisy measurements, generous negative space. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A sculptor robot chisels away a cloud of noisy data dots to reveal a clean simple curved law hidden inside, like a statue emerging from rough stone.
caption: Discovering dynamics is sculpture in reverse: chip away the noise and the few terms of the governing law are what remain standing.
